title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear a coordinator called Sarah talking about helping at a toy library. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear a coordinator called Sarah talking about helping at a toy library. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: A coordinator called Sarah describes helping at a toy library.
extracts.audio_script: Hello. I’m Sarah, coordinator of Bright Start Toy Library, where families can borrow toys and games in much the same way that people borrow books. Our organisation began with boxes kept in volunteers' homes, but that made it difficult for families to browse. We now operate from a converted [9]health clinic[/9], which was closed when new medical facilities opened nearby. The former waiting room is now a bright play area, and smaller rooms allow toys to be stored by age group and type.||Families pay a very small yearly membership charge and may borrow two items each week. We have puzzles, building sets, dressing-up clothing and outdoor equipment. At present, the item requested most frequently is our collection of [10]balance bikes[/10], which are useful for young children learning to move confidently before using ordinary bicycles. Volunteers are important because every item has to be checked between loans. Before anyone helps with children or toys, they attend training about [11]hygiene procedures[/11], including cleaning surfaces and separating damaged items from those ready to borrow.||New volunteers are never left to advise families alone on their first day. They usually begin by [12]checking puzzle pieces[/12], ensuring complete sets are returned before they go back onto shelves. This may sound simple, but it prevents frustration for the next child. All volunteers receive a name badge. People helping in the play area should wear comfortable clothes and bring [13]indoor shoes[/13], since outdoor footwear can make the floor muddy and children often sit or crawl there. There is safe storage for coats and personal belongings in the staff room.||We want families to enjoy using toys creatively. Once a month we offer a free activity session. Previous sessions have included simple music games and building challenges. This term, places filled fastest for our [14]storytelling morning[/14], when toys and puppets will help children act out a short story. We are also making borrowing easier for families unable to visit during opening hours. A new [15]online catalogue[/15] shows available toys and allows members to reserve an item before collecting it. Volunteers help photograph and describe toys clearly for this service.||Students who complete ten volunteering sessions receive a written [16]childcare reference[/16], as long as their work has been reliable. At the end of the year, families and volunteers are invited to a [17]play picnic[/17] in the park, bringing outdoor games from the library for everyone to share. Finally, many of our larger outdoor toys have become worn after heavy use. Donations collected during that event will allow us to buy a new [18]climbing frame[/18] for supervised activity sessions. Volunteer application forms and membership information are available on the desk by the entrance.

dialogue.speaker: sarah

questions.question: The toy library is now based in a former (9) .
questions.explanation: Sarah explains that the toy library uses a converted 'health clinic'.

questions.question: The item currently borrowed most often is (10) .
questions.explanation: Sarah identifies 'balance bikes' as most frequently requested.

questions.question: New volunteers normally begin by (12) .
questions.explanation: Sarah says new volunteers begin by 'checking puzzle pieces'.

questions.question: People helping in the play area should bring (13) .
questions.explanation: Sarah asks helpers to bring 'indoor shoes'.

questions.question: This term's quickest-filling activity is a (14) .
questions.explanation: Sarah says the 'storytelling morning' filled fastest.

questions.question: Families can reserve toys using the new (15) .
questions.explanation: Sarah explains that reservations are made through an 'online catalogue'.

questions.question: Reliable students completing ten sessions receive a (16) .

questions.question: Families and volunteers are invited to a (17) in the park.
Donations will help purchase a new (18) .
